successCriteria.description: File src/shared/types/leave.types.ts exists and exports exactly five TypeScript enums: LeaveType, LeaveStatus, NotificationType, AuditAction, EntityType
LeaveType enum has exactly six string members: ANNUAL, SICK, MATERNITY, PATERNITY, UNPAID, OTHER
LeaveStatus enum has exactly four string members: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
NotificationType enum has exactly six string members: LEAVE_REQUEST_CREATED, LEAVE_REQUEST_APPROVED, LEAVE_REQUEST_REJECTED, LEAVE_REQUEST_CANCELLED, LEAVE_BALANCE_LOW, LEAVE_BALANCE_EXPIRING
AuditAction enum has exactly three string members: CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE
EntityType enum has exactly five string members: LEAVE_REQUEST, LEAVE_BALANCE, LEAVE_POLICY, EMPLOYEE, NOTIFICATION
The file has zero import statements — no dependencies on any other project files

constraints: File must have zero imports — no dependencies on any other project files
All enums must be TypeScript string enums (explicit string values matching member names) to support serialization across the REST API and database layers
File must pass strict TypeScript compilation (no implicit any, strict null checks per HARNESS.json constraints.rules)
outOfScope: Phase 2 — Employee model, repository, and EmploymentStatus enum (src/modules/employee/)
Phases 6-10 — All service, controller, route, validation, and orchestration layers
Any database tables, migrations, or repository implementations
Unit tests for this file (no test file specified in this phase)

ambiguities.description: The enum is named LeaveType in Phase 1 but later phases (Phase 3 PLAN.md) reference it as LeaveTypeCode when importing from this file. Per the mandatory rules, this is expected cross-phase naming drift and not ambiguity — the Phase 1 name LeaveType is authoritative for this phase.
